study: Seymour et al. 2016 (Sepsis-3 Clinical Criteria Assessment)
population: Patients with suspected infection in ICU and non-ICU settings across 5 US and non-US data sets (UPMC, KPNC, VA, ALERTS, KCEMS)
predictor: SOFA score, qSOFA (quick SOFA), SIRS criteria, LODS score
outcome: In-hospital mortality; Secondary: In-hospital mortality or ICU length of stay ≥3 days
timing: Predictors measured near onset of suspected infection (from 48 hours before to 24 hours after); Outcome: In-hospital
method: Retrospective and prospective cohort studies; Multivariable logistic regression; AUROC analysis; Bayesian information criterion for qSOFA development
effect_size: 10-fold variation in mortality across deciles of baseline risk (0.7% to 8%); 3- to 11-fold increase in mortality for SOFA ≥2 vs <2 in ICU; up to 80-fold change for SOFA ≥2 vs <2 in non-ICU
performance: In ICU: SOFA AUROC=0.74 (95% CI, 0.73-0.76), LODS AUROC=0.75 (95% CI, 0.73-0.76), SIRS AUROC=0.64 (95% CI, 0.62-0.66). Outside ICU: qSOFA AUROC=0.70 (95% CI, 0.68-0.71)
mortality_rate: UPMC: 4%; KPNC: 5%; VA: 6%; ALERTS: 18%; KCEMS: 11%; ICU encounters (UPMC): 16-17%; Non-ICU encounters (UPMC): 3%
sofa_score: ICU encounters: Mean 6.2-6.3 (SD 3.9-4.0), Median 6 (IQR 3-9); Non-ICU encounters: Mean 1.4 (SD 1.9-2.0), Median 1 (IQR 0-2)
age: All encounters: Mean 61 years (SD 19); ICU: 62 (17); Non-ICU: 60-61 (20)
lactate: Measured in 57% of KPNC encounters; ≥2.0 mmol/L in 20-21% of ICU encounters and 2% of non-ICU encounters
icu_los: Secondary outcome defined as ICU LOS ≥3 days
hospital_los: Median 6 days (IQR 3-10) for all encounters; ICU: 12 days (7-19/20); Non-ICU: 6 days (3-9)
platelets: Included in SOFA and LODS (Table 1); Absent in 15% of non-ICU encounters
bilirubin: Included in SOFA and LODS (Table 1); Absent in 62% of non-ICU encounters
notes: Data extracted from Tables 1, 2, and 3, and Results text on pages 4-6. Performance metrics (AUROC) and score distributions (Figure 2) compare ICU vs. non-ICU settings. qSOFA was developed as a simpler bedside tool.
